reasoning: PayPal is approaching its Q3 2025 earnings on October 28. The stock has underperformed year-to-date and sits at a cheap valuation (forward P/E < 12) with average analyst price targets remaining solidly above current levels (~$80 compared to the current ~$68). Supported by a significant share buyback program ($6B targeted for the year out of $15B authorized), the risk/reward slightly favors upside as expectations appear quite low (e.g., recent Goldman Sachs downgrade). Consequently, I estimate a slightly higher-than-coinflip chance of the price being above the October 26 baseline on November 2. As per the instructions, the probabilities for the later milestones (November 25 and January 24) are tapered closer to 0.5 to reflect increasing uncertainty over longer horizons.
